title: `SystemError` related to `EmptyListStrategy_getitem` when calling list methods from threads
author: devdanzin
body:
First off, sorry for not being able to provide short repro code. I submit this in hopes that the error message or the long script may be enough for you to diagnose the issue.

Running the attached Python script sometimes results in an error like the one below. It's not clear to me which specific object has its methods being called across threads,

```python
START <Thread(Thread-425 (__str__), initial)>
STARTED <Thread(Thread-425 (__str__), started 139833882416896)>
START <Thread(Thread-426 (clear), initial)>
RPython traceback:
  File "pypy_interpreter.c", line 51609, in BuiltinCode1_fastcall_1
  File "implement_4.c", line 16589, in fastfunc_descr_repr_1_12
  File "pypy_objspace_std_3.c", line 4846, in listrepr
  File "rpython_jit_metainterp.c", line 20405, in ll_portal_runner__object_vtablePtr_object_vtable
  File "pypy_objspace_std_6.c", line 1900, in portal_20
  File "pypy_objspace_std_2.c", line 40284, in EmptyListStrategy_getitem
Exception in thread Thread-425 (__str__):
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/snap/pypy3/130/lib/pypy3.10/threading.py", line 1016, in _bootstrap_inner
    self.run()
  File "/snap/pypy3/130/lib/pypy3.10/threading.py", line 953, in run
    self._target(*self._args, **self._kwargs)
SystemError: unexpected internal exception (please report a bug): <IndexError object at 0x7f2da4e5fda8>; internal traceback was dumped to stderr
STARTED <Thread(Thread-426 (clear), stopped 139833908909824)>
```
[source_imp.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/17734942/source_imp.txt)

Found using fusil by @vstinner.

comments.author: cfbolz
comments.body: heh, amazing. yeah, I probably understand what is happening from the traceback alone.

comments.author: cfbolz
comments.body: the above commit should have fixed it. can't easily confirm however, because on my laptop your full script never crashed so far.

comments.author: devdanzin
comments.body: Sorry I didn't mention it, it doesn't crash, just prints the SystemError.
